prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG 1920s cosmic-horror atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired world map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye aged parchment atlas view of a vast cold ocean filled with many fogbound island groups, microcontinents, and hidden occult sites.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad dark ocean: several large fogbound islands, microcontinents, tiny haunted islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, craggy islands, fragmented rocky coasts, lighthouse islands, abandoned fishing villages, cult islands, drowned temple islands, ancient monolith islands, bleak tundra islands, dark pine forest islands, marsh islands, mountain islands, and impossible ruins.
Style/medium: 1920s New England town map expanded to archipelago scale, Lovecraftian horror, aged parchment with hand annotations but no readable text, occult cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with dark ocean border.
Lighting/mood: creeping cosmic dread, Jazz Age antiquarian mystery, fog, impossible shadows, hidden cult sea routes, ancient gods beneath the waves, sanity-eroding atmosphere.
Color palette: aged sepia parchment, cold grey-blue ocean, faded ink, sickly green occult accents, fog grey, dark pine forests, autumn browns, pale lighthouse glows.
Materials/textures: brittle paper, ink wash, etched coastlines, faint coffee stains, marginal occult sigils without readable lettering, dark waves and shoals.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
